articles.summary: Campaigns move fast. Teams are spread across time zones. There's never enough time for endless meetings or chasing down feedback.

articles.title: TIKTOK SHOP REACHES EBAY SALES VOLUME DESPITE REGULATORY HEADWINDS
articles.summary: TikTok Shop reached $19 billion in global quarterly sales, matching eBay's volume, as its US market grew 125% quarter-over-quarter despite regulatory pressure. The platform's rapid growth stems from its “discovery e-commerce” model, where 83% of shoppers find new products and 70% find new brands through shoppable videos and livestreams. Livestream shopping remains limited in the US, accounting for only 2% of traffic, although livestream sessions rose 72% year-over-year in September. Southeast Asia continues to outperform the US in adoption, while over 171,000 small businesses now sell on TikTok Shop as it tightens merchant vetting and expands across key retail categories.

articles.title: SMALL BUSINESS OPTIMISM TAKES A SMALL STEP BACK AS UNCERTAINTY EASES IN OCTOBER
articles.summary: Small business optimism fell slightly last month, with the NFIB Index dropping 0.6 points to 98.2 as owners reported lower sales and declining profits. Hiring remains difficult, with 32% of firms unable to fill job openings and 27% citing labor quality as their top problem. Inflation, supply chain issues, and rising costs continue to challenge businesses, though fewer cited financing or insurance as major problems.

articles.title: FAQ PAGES ARE COOLER THAN EVER
articles.summary: Adding FAQ sections to Webflow's template pages drove large traffic growth, especially from non-branded queries. Treated pages saw up to 8% lift while non-FAQ pages declined. FAQs captured high-intent, long-tail searches like “template website free” and increased AI search engagement. Clear and structured content can outperform traditional SEO tactics, making FAQs a powerful tool for discoverability.

articles.title: THE GROWTH STRATEGY MOST STARTUPS OVERLOOK: ENGINEERING-AS-MARKETING
articles.summary: Engineering-as-Marketing turns product or engineering time into a growth engine by creating free tools that attract and convert users through usefulness instead of promotion. It builds trust and organic reach by giving value upfront, leading to long-term traffic and compounding returns that paid ads cannot match. Examples like HubSpot's Website Grader, Shopify's Hatchful, and Ahrefs' free SEO tools show how offering helpful resources drives leads, backlinks, and brand awareness. Startups can apply this by building small, practical tools that solve real problems, launching them strategically, and optimizing for SEO to generate lasting growth.

articles.title: REACH MILLIONS OF TECH PROFESSIONALS AT SCALE
articles.summary: Over 6 million tech professionals read TLDR including developers, product managers, marketers, designers and executives. Get in front of your target audience with an ad placement just like this one! Learn more [9] about running a test campaign.

articles.title: HORSE
articles.summary: Horse Browser replaces tabs with a “Trails” system that organizes every link you open into a clear path in the sidebar. It helps users stay focused by tracking browsing history, bookmarks, and notes in one place. The browser includes ad blocking, a password manager, and dark mode. It aims to make web research more organized and less cluttered.

articles.title: WHEN AI GUIDES THE SHOPPING JOURNEY
articles.summary: About 40% of US consumers now use AI for shopping. Over 70% say AI anticipates their needs, 85% find its recommendations personalized, and 83% say it makes shopping more enjoyable. While AI helps people compare products and make decisions faster, 95% of shoppers still take extra steps to confirm what it suggests, showing a clear trust gap. Brands must prioritize accuracy, clear sourcing, and human validation to maintain confidence throughout the AI-shaped shopping journey.

articles.title: TURN EVERY EVENT INTO A GROWTH ENGINE WITH LINKEDIN EVENTS
articles.summary: LinkedIn introduced new enhancements to LinkedIn Events to help B2B marketers prove ROI, scale events efficiently, and reach the right audiences. Marketers using Event Ads see up to 31x more viewership, and brands such as Impact.com have doubled attendance. New integrations with ON24, Cvent, and Integrate allow event data, lead generation, and CRM connections to flow seamlessly for better targeting and measurement.

articles.title: THE SCAMMER NEXT DOOR
articles.summary: An investigation into India's scam economy shows how mass unemployment and inequality have turned fraud into a booming industry. What began with call center cons has expanded across social media, powered by underemployed youth who see deception as legitimate work. Using scripts and stolen data to target victims worldwide, they reflect a society where economic desperation and weak enforcement have blurred the line between ambition and deceit.

articles.title: WHY MAKE 1 AD WHEN YOU CAN MAKE 50?
articles.summary: With full creative control and minimal budget, Quality Meats delivered a 50-spot campaign for Mr. Submarine that combined scrappy execution with regional pride. The team used low-budget visuals, Chicago improv actors, and nostalgic jingles to create a series that was both chaotic and deeply ingrained in local culture. The initiative showed how unconventional ideas can be converted into high-impact community storytelling. Though not optimized for scale or efficiency, the campaign succeeded in generating buzz and reinforcing the brand's identity.

WHY THE BUZZIEST IPO IN HISTORY MAY NEVER HAPPEN
